description: A modular pipeline for transforming 2D geometric symbols (images or procedurally generated sigils) into higher-dimensional (X-D) representations via binary discretization, pattern analysis, fractal mapping, logical enforcement, and mathematical interpretation. Inspired by Adinkra diagrams in supersymmetry, radial symmetries, and connections to higher-dimensional physics (e.g., 12D supergravity/F-theory).

input_modes.description: User provides an external image file (PNG, JPG, BMP).
Procedural generation of a personal geometric sigil from user-provided numeric seed string.
input_modes.seed_input: String of digits or numbers (e.g., birthdate '19900101', constants '3.14159', or arbitrary sequence).

num_points.description: Number of anchor points for geometry.

input_modes.output: In-memory binary/grayscale image (cv::Mat) for direct pipeline feed.

dimensions_X.description: Target number of dimensions (X). Determines binary string length and grid size.
dimensions_X.recommended: Multiples of 8 (e.g., 8, 16, 32) or powers of 2 for alignment with byte boundaries and hypercube interpretations.
dimensions_X.note_on_accuracy: User observation that 'SymDim gets best results when number of dimensions equals bits in a byte' (i.e., multiples of 8) is partially supported: byte-aligned lengths simplify storage and hashing, but mathematically not required. In Adinkra physics, code length N (bits) equals the extension degree (number of supercharges), not tied to 8. Square grids (side = sqrt(X)) are preferred for perfect symmetry checks (horizontal/vertical/rotational). We recommend X as perfect squares that are multiples of 8 when possible (e.g., 16=4x4, 64=8x8) for optimal visual and analytical symmetry.

pipeline_steps.name: Image Acquisition & Preprocessing
actions: Optional edge detection/enhancement (cv::Canny or custom radial symmetry filter for sigils).
Threshold to binary (cv::threshold, adaptive if needed).

pipeline_steps.name: X-Dimensional Discretization
actions: Compute grid side length S = ceil(sqrt(X)).
Resize binary image to S x S (cv::resize, INTER_NEAREST for preservation).
Flatten row-major to X-bit binary string (pad with 0s if needed).
Store as std::bitset<X> or std::vector<bool> for efficiency.
pipeline_steps.output: Binary string of length X, optionally reshaped as S x S matrix for visualization.

pipeline_steps.name: Adinkra-Inspired Stylization (Optional Enhancement)
actions: Detect and amplify radial/rotational symmetry (polar transform or custom averaging).
Simplify to bold geometric glyph (morphological operations).

pipeline_steps.name: Deep Pattern Analysis
metrics: Symmetry checks: horizontal, vertical, 180° rotational, 90° if square.
Anomaly detection: flag asymmetry scores or unexpected entropy deviations.

pipeline_steps.name: L-System Mapping
pipeline_steps.output: Turtle graphics rendering of fractal rays.

pipeline_steps.name: Kripke Frame Enforcement
construction: Worlds = X nodes (one per bit position).
Accessibility relation: edge if positions adjacent in grid AND bits match.
enforced_properties: Invariant central cluster (AG p for hub).

pipeline_steps.name: Mathematical Display & Interpretation
outputs: Sample equations (e.g., Weierstrass form for elliptic fibration ties, supergravity action templates).
Profound notes linking to physics (optional toggle).

rules_for_mathematical_accuracy.grid_shape: Prefer square grids (X perfect square) for unbiased rotational symmetry checks.
rules_for_mathematical_accuracy.bit_alignment: Multiples of 8 recommended for practical byte handling and hashing, but not mathematically mandatory.
rules_for_mathematical_accuracy.padding: If X not perfect square, pad with 0s transparently and note in report.
